expectation: Listen to and interact with peers during social interactions; read‐alouds; and class, group, and partner discussions.

expectation: Use gestures and expressions to demonstrate engagement and understanding in a socially appropriate manner.

expectation: Listen and respond to simple commands and instructions or directions with 3 or more steps.

expectation: Listen and respond to simple 5W and 1H questions.

expectation: Participate in class, group, and partner discussions by listening attentively, following turn-taking rules, and asking and answering questions about stories and personal experience with increasingly elaborated responses, transitioning from one- or two-word responses to phrases and simple sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Respond orally to closed- and open-ended questions.

expectation: Evaluate information and determine appropriate responses to answer questions effectively.

expectation: Exchange common social greetings, retell texts, and recount experiences using complete sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use words, phrases, and short sentences to express ideas for a variety of purposes (e.g., to communicate needs and desires, express feelings).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Expand sentences to provide details (e.g., Who? What? When?) about a familiar or new activity or process.

expectation: Contribute to social, academic, college, and career conversations using accurate and appropriate language.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Respond orally to conversations, read-alouds, texts, and oral presentations using a growing number of general, academic, and content-specific words and relying less on physical actions or other means of nonverbal communication.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Provide, justify, and defend opinions or positions in speech.

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, express opinions using an expanded set of learned phrases and recalling textual evidence or relevant background knowledge.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Share and elaborate on statements, opinions, or arguments using language models or sentence starters (prompts).

expectation: Adjust language choices according to the task, context, purpose, and audience.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Describe personal experiences using new vocabulary and details about familiar topics appropriate to the situation.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.

expectation: Plan and deliver different types of oral presentations/reports to express information and support ideas in social, academic, college, and career settings.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Plan and deliver brief oral presentations on a variety of familiar topics using illustrations, objects, words, phrases, or vocabulary from a text (e.g., integrating print or technological/digital resources).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Retell literary (familiar stories) or informational texts using newly acquired vocabulary and details appropriate to the situation.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recite, memorize, or present rhymes, poems, or songs. Foundational Skills The Foundational Skills Standard is aimed at fostering the student’s understanding and working knowledge of print concepts, the alphabetic principle, and other basic conventions of the English writing system. These foundational skills are not independent objectives; rather, they are necessary and important components of an effective, comprehensive reading program. Instruction should be differentiated: good readers will need much less practice with these concepts than struggling readers. The objective is to discern when students need attention in these skills.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Print concepts

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.

expectation: Recognize that the alphabet consists of consonants and vowels.

expectation: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.

expectation: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

expectation: Recognize that print conveys specific meaning and that pictures may support meaning.

expectation: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).

expectation: Phonological awareness (rhyming, blending, segmentation, deletion, isolation, substitution)

expectation: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

expectation: Recognize and match rhyming words, including nonsense words.

expectation: Identify syllables through actions (e.g., clapping).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Manipulate sounds and words in shared, guided, and independent activities, such as singing, chanting, or participating in finger plays.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken monosyllabic words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Count and orally produce monosyllabic words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (3 to 5 phonemes) in spoken monosyllabic (consonant-vowel-consonant or CVC, and variants) words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) with 3 phonemes in simple monosyllabic words to make new words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Segment spoken monosyllabic words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Phonics and word recognition

expectation: Understand and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills to decode short and long sound patterns and their variants both in isolation and in text.

expectation: Understand the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.

expectation: Decode regularly spelled monosyllabic words.

expectation: Understand final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.

expectation: Determine the number of syllables in a printed word using the knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound.

expectation: Decode two- to three-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.

expectation: Read words with inflectional endings.

expectation: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

expectation: Read common (50–100) high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Fluency

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension. (See fluency assessment tools in the appendix.)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read on-level texts orally with accuracy and appropriate rate and expression on successive readings.

subject: Inglés
expectation: With prompting and support, make predictions, describe ideas, phenomena (e.g., how butterflies eat), textual elements (e.g., setting, characters), and key details based on understanding a variety of grade-level and read-aloud texts or multimedia resources. Determine main ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development. Summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

subject: Inglés
expectation: .1L Retell familiar stories, including the main idea and key details at grade level.

expectation: Analyze the structure of a text, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., sections, chapters, scenes, or stanzas) relate to each other and to the whole.

expectation: Identify the differences between literary and informational texts.

expectation: Identify rhyme in a poem. Analyze how and why individuals, events, or ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: .1L With prompting and support, describe characters, settings, and major events in a story or from a read-aloud using key details.

expectation: .1L Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a literary text or use illustrations to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a literary text.

expectation: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

expectation: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use illustrations (picture cues) to identify or describe story details and categorize similarities and differences between characters and details within a variety of grade level texts (e.g., nursery rhymes, folktales, etc.) or when viewing multimedia resources.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use titles, headings, and illustrations to predict and confirm text topics.

expectation: Delineate and evaluate an author’s argument through evidence specified in a text.

subject: Inglés
expectation: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.

expectation: Compare and contrast 2 or more authors’ presentations of similar themes or topics.

expectation: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Read literary texts (e.g., folktale, realistic fiction, animal fantasy, legends, fairytales, poetry) and informational (e.g., science or social science articles) with purpose and understanding. The Writing Standard develops the student’s capacity and skills to plan, revise, edit, and publish many types of writing, such as arguments, informational/explanatory texts, and narratives. This standard stresses the importance of the reading-writing connection by requiring students to draw upon and write about evidence from literary and informational texts/media or when writing for research and investigations. Write arguments to support point of view using valid reasoning and relevant evidence.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write simple sentences (subject + verb) using high-frequency words and illustrations to express opinions and feelings or describe a picture, person, or object. Write informational texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With increasing independence, use a combination of simple sentences (subject + verb), copying, and drawing to compose short informational texts using high-frequency words collaboratively with a teacher or peers. Write literary texts to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, details, and structure.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With increasing independence, use a combination of simple sentences (subject + verb), copying, and drawing to compose short literary texts collaboratively with a teacher or peers.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, express opinions about a topic or text with at least one supporting reason. Develop and strengthen writing as needed by using the writing process (planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, and publishing).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, brainstorm ideas, and use feedback on a topic (e.g., additional texts, drawings, visual displays, labels) to strengthen writing. Use technology, including the internet, to interact and collaborate with others and produce and publish writing.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use a multimedia element to enhance a written task. Multimedia elements may include, but are not limited to, drawings, pictures, artifacts, and audio or digital representations. At this grade level, the multimedia element should relate to the task.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Conduct research projects of varying lengths based on focused questions to demonstrate understanding of the subject.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Participate in shared research to gather information about a topic (e.g., drawings, visual displays, labels).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore several how-to books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use experiences to gather information from a variety of sources (e.g., word walls, book talks, weather charts, routine tasks) to answer a question.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write routinely over short and extended periods of time for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: With guidance and support, use a combination of drawing, dictating, invented and creative writing, and/or labeling a picture with a few words following a prompt. Language The Language Standard is presented as a summary of the skills and abilities that support the understanding of essential rules of written and spoken English. The vocabulary focuses on understanding and acquiring new general, academic, and content-specific words and phrases.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English grammar and usage conventions.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English grammar and usage conventions when writing or speaking.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use common and proper nouns.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form singular and plural nouns that agree with verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops. / We hop.).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring regular verbs (e.g., want/wanted, play/played).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home. / Today I walk home. / Tomorrow I will walk home.).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use frequently occurring adjectives. 1LA.1.1h Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., in, on, under, in front, behind, next to).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Produce and expand complete simple sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Apply appropriate English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling conventions.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate command of English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling conventions when writing.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Capitalize the first word in a sentence, dates, and names of people.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use a period for end punctuation of sentences.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Write a letter or letters for short and long vowel sounds (phonemes).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use conventional spelling for basic sight words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words (e.g., said = /s/ /e/ /d/).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Consult reference materials, including picture and standard dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spelling.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of how language functions in different contexts, making effective choices for meaning, style, and comprehension.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Choose words and phrases for different purposes (e.g., slang, written vs. spoken, formal vs. informal).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting reference materials.

expectation: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on class reading and content area study, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use context clues and illustrations to identify the meaning of unfamiliar words including compound words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Add prefixes to words and determine new meaning (e.g., happy/unhappy, view/review).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use basic phonemic awareness strategies to decode words.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and variation in word meanings.

subject: Inglés
expectation: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and differences in word meanings.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Recognize, sort, and categorize words (e.g., colors, clothing) to show comprehension. Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims, a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Make and apply connections between words and their usage including synonyms and antonyms (e.g., big park, small room).
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Accurately use a variety of social, academic, and content-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career-readiness level.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)

subject: Inglés
expectation: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations and read-alouds. Listening/Speaking The Listening/Speaking Standard develops a range of broadly useful oral communication and interpersonal skills, including those used for formal or informal presentations. The student learns to communicate, collaborate, and listen to ideas; strategically integrate information from oral, visual quantitative, and media sources to evaluate what they hear, use, and display, helping to achieve communicative purposes, and adapt speech to context and task.
source: DEPR Estándares de Contenido y Expectativas de Grado — Inglés (2022)
